disclaimer: Live music schedules change frequently. Always check the venue's social media or call ahead to confirm tonight's lineup.

venues.name: Blue Moon Saloon & Guesthouse
venues.vibe: Historic 1900s home turned iconic music venue & boutique guesthouse. Intimate, eclectic, nationally acclaimed.
venues.notes: Voted one of the Best Bars in America by Esquire. Happy hour 5 PM with $3 Crown, $3 Jameson, $4 local beers. Check live music calendar online for full schedule.

venues.name: Artmosphere
venues.vibe: Artist-focused bistro by day, lively live music venue by night. Covered in local artwork, large patio, full bar with specialty cocktails.
venues.notes: Live music 7 days a week. One of Acadiana's top indie music venues. Located at 902 Johnston St, Lafayette. Bottomless brunch available.

venues.name: Randol's Restaurant & Cajun Dance Hall

musicNights.description: Live Cajun music and dancing (e.g. Maegan Benoit)

venues.vibe: Classic Acadiana dance hall with large dance floor. Open 40+ years. Locals and tourists two-stepping nightly. Great crawfish.
venues.notes: Located at 2320 Kaliste Saloom Rd, Lafayette. Known for crawfish by the pound. Live music nightly; call ahead to confirm specific nights.

venues.name: Prejean's Restaurant
venues.vibe: Beloved Cajun restaurant with dance floor and live music every night. Home to Big Al, a 14-foot stuffed alligator. Family-friendly.
venues.notes: Located at 3480 NE Evangeline Thruway (I-49 N), Lafayette. Famous for gumbo, wild game, and seafood. Reservations available via OpenTable.

venues.name: Rock'n'Bowl de Lafayette

musicNights.description: Zydeco Sunday – live zydeco starting around 5 PM

venues.vibe: Unique combo of bowling alley and live music venue. Dance floor in the lanes. Restaurant and full bar. High-energy crowd.
venues.notes: Check live music calendar on website for full schedule. Has hosted touring acts like Marc Broussard.

venues.name: Buck & Johnny's

musicNights.description: World-Famous Zydeco Breakfast – live zydeco music while you dine

venues.vibe: Cajun-Italian restaurant in a restored motor building. Famous Saturday Zydeco Breakfast with a balcony overlooking the dance floor. First come, first served.
venues.notes: 100 Berard St, Breaux Bridge. No reservations for Zydeco Breakfast – doors open at 8 AM. Yvette Landry and Richard Comeaux have standing residency Thu–Sat evenings.

venues.name: La Poussière Cajun Dancehall
venues.vibe: Authentic Cajun dancehall open since 1955. Large dance floor, welcoming locals, inexpensive beer. A true cultural experience.
venues.notes: Grand Point Hwy area, Breaux Bridge. One of the oldest and most beloved Cajun dance halls in Acadiana. Very local crowd.

venues.name: Fred's Lounge

musicNights.description: Live Cajun music broadcast on local radio – beer, boudin, dancing

venues.vibe: World-famous Saturday morning institution. Beer and boudin at 7 AM. Live Cajun music broadcast on local radio. Legendary Acadiana experience.
venues.notes: 420 6th Street, Mamou. (318) 468-5411. Doors open 7 AM Saturdays, music 9 AM–noon. One of the most unique live music experiences in the South.

venues.name: Liberty Theater

musicNights.description: Rendezvous des Cajuns – live Cajun music radio/TV show in French, with dance floor

venues.vibe: Renovated 1924 vaudeville house. Weekly Cajun music radio and TV show hosted in French. Dance floor open to attendees. Cultural landmark.
venues.notes: 200 W Park Ave, Eunice. Part of the Eunice Louisiana prairie Cajun music trail alongside Savoy Music Center and Fred's Lounge.

venues.name: Savoy Music Center

musicNights.description: Open acoustic jam session – anyone can bring an instrument and join in

venues.cover: Free (donation of boudin or cracklins welcome)
venues.vibe: Accordion shop and cultural institution. 40+ years of Saturday morning jams. Grassroots Cajun music community experience.
venues.notes: 337-580-0899. Open Saturdays 9 AM–noon for 40+ years. Owned by accordion-maker Marc Savoy. A bucket-list Cajun music experience.

venues.name: El Sido's Zydeco & Blues Club
venues.vibe: Legendary zydeco club operating since 1984. Has hosted Clifton Chenier, Buckwheat Zydeco, Rockin' Dopsie, Nathan & the Zydeco Cha-Chas. Authentic and historic.
venues.notes: St. Antoine & Martin Luther King Dr., Lafayette. Est. 1984. Check Facebook for current schedule.

venues.name: Angelle's Whiskey River Landing

musicNights.description: Live Cajun and zydeco music overlooking the Atchafalaya Basin

venues.vibe: Rustic dance hall on the banks of the Atchafalaya Basin levee. Stunning swamp views. Established 1982. Every Sunday afternoon.
venues.notes: 1365 Henderson Levee Rd, Breaux Bridge area. Open every Sunday 3–9 PM. Started as a bar and boat launch. One of the most scenic music spots in Louisiana.

venues.name: Feed N Seed
venues.vibe: Rustic feed-store-themed venue with antique décor. Large open dance floor for fais do-do (traditional Cajun dancing). Outdoor patio.
venues.notes: Downtown Lafayette area. Check Facebook for current schedule and events.

venues.name: The Grouse Room
venues.vibe: Downtown Lafayette's premier nightclub and live music venue. State-of-the-art sound and lighting, built-in stage, balcony, courtyard. Craft cocktails.
venues.notes: Heart of downtown Lafayette. Features local and touring artists. Bar food available. Dueling pianos events held periodically.

venues.name: Pont Breaux's Cajun Restaurant
venues.vibe: Formerly Mulate's – the original Cajun restaurant. Authentic Cajun food and live music. Known for seafood and traditional recipes.
venues.notes: 325 W Mills Ave, Breaux Bridge. (337) 332-4648. Formerly operated as Mulate's Cajun Restaurant; same tradition of nightly live music. Call ahead to verify current schedule.

venues.name: Slim's Y-Ki-Ki

musicNights.description: Live zydeco (when open – verify before visiting)
Live zydeco (when open – verify before visiting)

venues.vibe: Legendary historic zydeco dance hall on Hwy 182/167. Iconic venue that hosted Clifton Chenier, Rockin' Dopsie, and Boozoo Chavis. Currently undergoing restoration.
venues.notes: Hwy 182/167, Opelousas. (318) 942-9980. NOTE: As of 2024, this venue is undergoing restoration. Call ahead or check social media to confirm current status before visiting.

venues.name: Richard's Club
venues.vibe: Legendary Zydeco roadhouse on Hwy 190 between Lawtell and Opelousas. Renowned for epic zydeco dances. Authentic and local.
venues.notes: Hwy 190, 3–4 miles west of Opelousas. Proprietor Kelvin Richard. (318) 543-6596. Verify schedule before visiting.

venues.name: Cafe Sydnie Mae

musicNights.description: Live music during brunch and evening service

venues.vibe: Charming downtown Breaux Bridge café with live local music and art. Modern Cajun cuisine. Warm, community feel.
venues.notes: 140 E Bridge St, Breaux Bridge. Live music on weekends. Also doubles as a brunch destination.
